rationale: Ran the mandated grep for 'refutation_discharged|discharge|re-tally|resolves naturally|natural resolution' across ADR-0037, plugins/mindspec/skills/ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md, and .claude/skills/ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md — zero hits. A broader sweep for 'discharg|re-tall|retally|dischargeEvidence|origin/Panels|verified.discharge' also returned nothing. The only textually-related hits (ADR line 327, both SKILL.md copies line 94) are the explicitly-acceptable rationale phrase: 'the audit is written from that record, never by re-reading mutable panel files' / 'not by re-reading panel files' — explaining why there's no re-tally, not describing one. Read ADR §7 (lines 294-348) and §8 carve-out (378-410) in full: they describe a self-contained refutation_pending marker, UNION-persist, fail-closed metadata, and satisfy-from-marker reconciliation — matching the shipped no-discharge design, not the deleted discharge-by-re-tally path. 'Natural resolution' (re-panel where dissenter flips, no refutation applied → no marker, zero ceremony) is described in ADR line 345-348 without ever using a 'discharge' label. No other deleted symbols (dischargeEvidence, origin/Panels) found. Mirror confirmed byte-identical via diff. git status clean at 38ee213c.

findings.note: Acceptable rationale phrase per BRIEF: 'the audit is written from that record, never by re-reading mutable panel files, so it cannot be spoofed by panel-file manipulation.' Explains absence of re-tally; does not reintroduce a discharge/re-tally concept. No action required.
Same acceptable rationale: '...not by re-reading panel files'. Describes satisfy-from-marker audit, not a verified-discharge or re-tally mechanism. No action required.

grep for 'refutation_discharged', 'discharge', 're-tally', 'resolves naturally', 'natural resolution', 'dischargeEvidence', 'origin/Panels', 'verified discharge' returned no matches describing a deleted mechanism. No stale references found.
